chapter_title.en: Chapter on the mutamatti‘ Leaving Mecca and Returning

text: 2753 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : هَلْ يَدْخُلُ اَلرَّجُلُ مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ إِحْرَامٍ قَالَ «لاَ إِلاَّ مَرِيضٌ أَوْ مَنْ بِهِ بَطَنٌ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2753 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Abu Ja'far (as): "Can a person enter Mecca without being in the state of ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "No, except for someone who is ill or has a stomach ailment."
